summary: Alfred is an architecturally intentional Rust text editor with a clear functional-core / imperative-shell split enforced at crate boundaries. The codebase has outstanding documentation: a thorough root README.md, 8 ADRs covering every major decision, comprehensive per-module Rustdoc, 2 walkthrough documents, and detailed feature design docs. The main comprehensibility risk is size concentration in three mega-files (app.rs at 6882 lines, bridge.rs at 5669 lines, editor_state.rs at 2652 lines) that are simultaneously the most complex and most frequently changed files. A new developer can understand the system architecture quickly from the ADRs and README but will face a steep cliff when they need to trace through key dispatch or Lisp bridge registration.

top_hotspots.notes: Most frequently changed file in git history (62 commits). 6882 lines mixing terminal event loop, key conversion, operator/motion dispatch (InputState, Operator enums), deferred action execution, and gutter/status bar hook dispatch. High cyclomatic complexity from nested match arms in handle_key_event. Core domain logic (operator-pending state machine) is buried here instead of in alfred-core.

5669 lines of Lisp primitive registrations. Each of 35+ primitives is a closure capturing Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> with direct access to 5+ core submodules. Understanding any bridge primitive requires tracing through RefCell borrow patterns and the Lisp Value enum. 43 commits indicates high ongoing churn as new primitives are added.

2652 lines. The EditorState struct (40+ public fields) is the single most coupled type in the system -- any feature addition touches this file. 46 commits places it as the second most changed file. Recent additions: panels, line_styles, focused_panel, cli_argument, change_list. The god-object pattern makes it increasingly difficult to reason about subsystem invariants.

recommendations.title: Decompose app.rs into focused modules
recommendations.description: alfred-tui/src/app.rs is 6882 lines and contains the event loop, key conversion, InputState/Operator state machine, motion resolution, deferred action execution, and hook dispatch. Split into at minimum: event_loop.rs (I/O boundary), input_machine.rs (pure InputState transitions), and operator.rs (motion/operator domain logic). Move InputState and Operator enums to alfred-core to enable unit testing without the TUI layer. This is the highest-leverage single change for maintainability.

recommendations.title: Decompose EditorState into focused sub-structs
recommendations.description: EditorState has 40+ public fields covering unrelated concerns. Group into sub-structs: ModalState (mode, selection_start, visual_line_mode), MacroState (macro_recording, macro_buffer, macro_registers, macro_replaying), SearchState (search_pattern, search_forward, last_char_find), NavigationState (jump_list, jump_index, change_list, change_list_index), and RenderState (line_styles, line_backgrounds, panels). Each sub-struct can be independently understood, tested, and extended without touching unrelated fields.

recommendations.title: Introduce typed domain event constants to replace stringly-typed hooks
recommendations.description: The HookRegistry uses string hook names ('render-gutter', 'on-save', 'render-status') with no compile-time validation. Define a HookName module in alfred-core with string constants (or a typed enum) for all known hook names. This prevents silent typo failures, makes hook contracts discoverable, and documents the extension surface for plugin authors.

recommendations.title: Move rainbow_csv out of alfred-core into a plugin or alfred-syntax
recommendations.description: crates/alfred-core/src/rainbow_csv.rs implements a feature plugin concern (CSV colorization) inside the pure domain kernel. It belongs either in alfred-syntax (syntax-adjacent) or as a native bridge helper in alfred-lisp alongside the rainbow-csv Lisp plugin. Its presence in alfred-core violates the single-responsibility of the kernel and adds an unnecessary concern to the shared foundation.

recommendations.title: Migrate bridge.rs primitives to use the facade layer
recommendations.description: bridge.rs imports 5+ alfred-core submodules directly (buffer, cursor, command, hook, theme, viewport, panel). With the existing facade.rs already providing controlled access, updating bridge.rs to use only facade:: calls would reduce the coupling surface from 5 imports to 1 and make bridge.rs resilient to internal alfred-core restructuring.
